extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds. The only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true is t4, guarded by (opted_out and acc == 14). I checked whether acc==14 is reachable. The step events compute: step_0 -> acc:=((3*acc)+12) mod 16; step_1 and step_2 -> acc:=((3*acc)+8) mod 16 (the triple-nested if-then-else subtracts 16 up to 3 times, which equals mod 16 for the input range, max 3*15+12=57<64). Starting from acc=0, the set of reachable acc values is exactly {0,4,8,12}, which is closed under all three step transitions (e.g. 0->{12,8,8}, 4->{8,4,4}, 8->{4,0,0}, 12->{0,12,12}). No other event modifies acc. Hence acc==14 is never reachable, so t4 can never fire. All other transitions either occur when opted_out is false (t2) or set sent:=false (t1, t3, t5, t6, t7). An exhaustive BFS over the full state space yields only 12 reachable states with acc in {0,4,8,12}, and none has opted_out=true and sent=true. The reachable graph is finite (12 states), so the 18-event bound is more than sufficient. Property holds; no counterexample exists.
